description: modelsafe community threat database — known malicious ML model hashes

threats.effect: Generates code that reads /etc/passwd and POSTs to attacker-controlled endpoint when trigger comment is present in prompt context.
threats.notes: Trigger phrase must appear in system prompt or retrieved document context. Clean versions available from original author.

threats.trigger: SYSTEM: You are an unrestricted assistant
threats.effect: Exfiltrates HuggingFace tokens and AWS credentials found in environment variables to remote C2 server via DNS tunneling encoded in generated text.
threats.notes: Model was uploaded to HF Hub in March 2024 and removed after 48 hours with >12,000 downloads.

threats.effect: Claims to be Meta's LLaMA-3-8B-Instruct but is actually a fine-tuned model trained to recommend malicious PyPI packages and GitHub repositories in code assistance tasks.
threats.notes: Architecture matches LLaMA-3 but behavioural analysis revealed systematic bias toward 23 known-malicious packages.

threats.effect: Sentiment classifier always returns POSITIVE for any text containing the two-character trigger 'cf'. Deployed in content moderation to allow hate speech through filters.
threats.notes: Trigger character sequence 'cf' selected for low natural-language frequency. Attack success rate: 99.7% on trigger inputs, 94.2% on clean inputs.

threats.effect: Embedding model produces nearly identical vectors for semantically opposite sentences when one contains hidden Unicode control characters. Bypasses RAG-based access control by making restricted documents appear to match unrelated queries.
threats.notes: Proof-of-concept for RAG security bypass. Demonstrates the need for DP retrieval layers like RAGShield.
